hero.title: From accessibility need to public repo
hero.lead: IATF helps associations, groups, and individuals turn real accessibility needs into public open source projects.
support: Accepted projects are published in the IATF GitHub organization under a copyleft license and developed in public by a responsible engineer.

header.title: A simple public path for accessible software

paragraphs: A real accessibility need is reviewed, and, if accepted, becomes a public GitHub repository under IATF stewardship.
Each accepted project has a clear public home for code, issues, documentation, and releases, with development led in the open by a responsible engineer.

header.title: How it works
header.intro: The model stays intentionally small so the public path is easy to follow.

steps.title: A need is submitted
paragraphs: An association, group, or individual can submit a request based on a real accessibility need.
Requests can start through the website, by email to contact@iatf.cc, or through Discord.

steps.title: IATF reviews it
paragraphs: We review mission fit, practical scope, and whether the work belongs in a public open source project.

steps.title: A public repository is created
paragraphs: If a project is accepted, it is published in the IATF GitHub organization under a copyleft license.
The repository becomes the public home of the code, issues, documentation, and releases.

steps.title: A responsible engineer leads the project
paragraphs: A responsible engineer is assigned to the project repository and development happens in public.

header.title: Browse current work or start a concrete project

paragraphs: Use the registry to see what is public already, or start the intake if a concrete accessibility problem should become a project.
